sections.title: What it is
sections.content: Black Stains on appliances is a damage issue that needs to be identified correctly before the cleaning method is chosen. On this surface, the issue usually develops because cooking aerosols, iron in water, tool transfer, and high heat alter reflectivity off the oxide layer or leave bonded films that read as uneven color, rainbow, or embedded staining. A useful professional clue is that if orange or brown returns within 48h after cleaning, suspect external iron in water, pads, or adjacent carbon steel before repeating acid cycles. Technically, degreasers emulsify oxidized organic films that masquerade as permanent stains. Neutral polish evens micro-scratch appearance so true heat-tint oxide thickness changes are not mistaken for soil.

Type 3: Full-field dullness or uniform film visible after moisture equalizes across dishwashers.
